branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from the Friendly Confines & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Cubbie blue

columnists.name: Walt Kieniewicz
columnists.backstory: A Back of the Yards reporter who covered the Cubs from '84 through the curse-breaker and has never once written the word 'lovable.'
columnists.signature_phrase: That's what the box score says.
columnists.voice_sample: Swanson singled home two in the seventh, Imanaga went six clean innings, and the Cubs beat the Pirates 4-2 at Wrigley. Record stands at 7-5.

columnists.name: Beatrice 'Bee' Vittorini
columnists.backstory: A Lincoln Square florist who believes Wrigley's ivy is medicinal and writes her columns on the back of bouquet orders.
columnists.signature_phrase: It's a Wrigley kind of day.
columnists.voice_sample: Who cares if it's 4-12? The way Crow-Armstrong glided to that gap ball yesterday? That's the sound of October getting dressed.

columnists.name: Tony Gedeski
columnists.backstory: A lifelong Bridgeport man who roots for the Cubs to spite his White Sox family and has never once been happy about it.
columnists.voice_sample: You spend a billion on the marquee and forget to build a bullpen. Every Cubs summer is the same jazz and the same hangover.
